text: In *Referendums Around the World*, Dr. Matt Qvortrup and a global team of legal and political experts provide a definitive account of why and when citizens are called upon to vote on specific policy issues. Surveying the historical landscape of direct democracy from 1793 to the present day, the contributors analyze the evolution and continued growth of the referendum across every continent. From the long-standing traditions of Switzerland and the United States to the emerging uses of ballot measures in Africa, Asia, and Latin America, this comprehensive volume offers a thorough examination of the mechanisms, motivations, and outcomes that define modern plebiscitary politics. This updated study serves as an essential resource for understanding the diverse global experience of popular sovereignty.
